title: AST Officer Involved Shooting Investigation: Bethel [AK26026271]
summary: At the request of the Bethel Police Department, the Alaska Bureau of Investigation is investigating an officer-involved shooting that occurred in Bethel on March 25, 2026, at approximately 4:05 am. A preliminary investigation indicates an adult male had been taken to the Bethel hospital earlier in the night after standing in the roadway and refusing to move onto the sidewalk. The man left the hospital before being seen and later returned to the roadway. Officers contacted the man a second time, at which point he produced a knife and refused multiple commands to drop it. The man then approached an officer while brandishing the knife, and the officer fired, striking him. The man was medevaced to an Anchorage-area hospital for treatment of serious injuries. No other injuries were reported to
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Wasilla [AK26026211]
summary: On 03/24/2026 at approximately 2255 hours, the Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop on a black 2002 Mercedes-Benz C230 near Knik-Goose Bay Road and Eagle Eye Circle for speeding and swerving in its lane. The vehicle failed to stop, leading Troopers on a 20-mile pursuit with speeds reaching 125mph. The vehicle crashed at the end of Knik-Goose Bay Road near the Goose Bay airstrip and the driver fled on foot into the woods. Troopers located the driver, identified as Trinton Spohnholz, age 21 of Anchorage, hiding under a fallen tree and arrested him without further incident. Trinton was remanded at MSPT for Failure to Stop 1 and Reckless Driving and held with no bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Residential Fire Fatality: Fairbanks [AK26025408]
summary: On March 22, 2026, at 1:38pm, University Fire Department received a report of a residential trailer fire in Goldrush Estates in Fairbanks. On arrival crews found heavy fire involvement throughout the trailer. Information was received from neighbors that the structure was likely occupied at the time of the fire. During a primary search of the structure, fire crews found and extracted a male adult with significant burn injuries. He was transported to a local area hospital where he succumbed to his burn injuries. Department of Public Safety Deputy Fire Marshals from Fairbanks responded to conduct and origin and cause investigation. The body is being transported to the State Medical Examiner's Office for confirmation of identification and autopsy. No foul play is suspected at this time.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

summary: On March 20, 2026, the Alaska State Troopers assisted the Sandusky County Ohio Sheriff's Office. James was remanded to the Fairbanks Correctional Center.

title: AST DV / Arson: Stebbins [AK26024115]
summary: On March 18, 2026, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a residential fire in Stebbins. Investigation revealed the fire stemmed from a domestic violence disturbance inside the home. During the incident, 43-year-old Connie Acoman intentionally set the walls of the residence on fire. At the time, two adults and two small children were inside the home and were placed in danger. After starting the fire, Acoman fled onto the Bering Sea, sea ice, where she was later detained by Stebbins VPOs. AST from Nome responded to Stebbins and took custody of Acoman. She was remanded to AMCC without further incident.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Commercial Structure Fire: Cordova [AK21133223]
summary: On 11/20/2021, at approximately 2200 hours, the Cordova Fire Department responded to a commercial structure fire in Cordova, Alaska. The commercial warehouse, Alpine Diesel, which housed a diesel mechanic shop on one side and a fiberglass repair shop on the other, was heavily engulfed upon arrival. Two Deputy Fire Marshals traveled to Cordova to conduct an origin and cause investigation. The interior examination was limited due to the significant structural instability compounded with recent heavy snowfall. The value of the structure and its contents were in excess of $4 million. No foul play is suspected at this time. Investigation is continuing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

entities.address_detail: was limited due to the significant structural instability compounded with recent heavy sno

title: AST Residential Fire / Fatality: Anchor Point [AK21134472]
summary: On 11/25/2021, at approximately 1910 hours, AST was notified of a residential fire in the Anchor Point area. The Anchor Point Fire Department and Troopers responded to find the home fully engulfed in fire. One occupant of the home was discovered deceased inside while a second resident was found at a nearby residence and has since been transported to Seattle for medical treatment due to injuries sustained in the fire. State Fire Marshals are continuing to investigate the cause of the fire.

title: AST Shooting / Road Rage Investigation: Ester [AK21128806]
summary: On November 8, 2021, at approximately 1842 hours, Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks were notified that a Ford SUV had rammed and shot at a Jeep SUV near Ester. Alaska State Troopers immediately responded and contacted the adult female driver and sole occupant of the Jeep SUV on the Parks Highway. Her vehicle had been severely damaged and contained multiple bullet holes, the driver was not injured during the incident. A preliminary investigation revealed that the female driver was rammed multiple times by the Ford SUV. The driver of the Ford SUV repeatedly shot a semiautomatic rifle at the Jeep. title: AST Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance 4th Degree, False Information: Palmer [AK21126585]
incident_type: Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance 4th Degree, False Information
summary: On 11/2/21, at approximately 1818 hours, the Alaska State Troopers, Palmer Post, received a report of a man attempting to break into a residence near Old Trunk Road. An AST K-9 team responded and contacted Timothy Mitchell, age 35 of Wasilla, walking in the area. Investigation revealed Mitchell was in possession of methamphetamine and had been twice previously convicted of misconduct involving a controlled substance. Further investigation revealed Mitchell provided several false names when contacted by law enforcement. Mitchell was placed under arrest for Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance the 4th Degree, and providing false information to a law enforcement officer. Mitchell was transported to MSPT where he was held without bail.

title: AST SAR: Emmonak [AK21125234]
summary: Update 11/5: On November 4, 2021, at approximately 1900 hours the US Coast Guard was able to launch a helicopter from Nome to extract the group. At approximately 2040 hours, the group was successfully extracted from the fish camp and transported to Nome by the USCG helicopter. There were no injuries reported. The Alaska State Troopers would like to thank our partners at the US Coast Guard, US Army Alaska, the Alaska Rescue Coordination Center, and Grant Aviation for their assistance during this operation. Update 11/4: The Alaska State Troopers and our search and rescue partners at the US Coast Guard and US Army are continuing to evaluate every available option to safely extract the seven individuals that are iced in approximately 20 miles east of Emmonak. title: AST Terroristic Threatening 2: Palmer [AK21107058]
summary: Final update: On 09/14/2021, after continued investigation the suspect’s identity is still unconfirmed. Federal law enforcement officials have declined to pursue federal criminal charges. On 09/13/2021 at approximately 0950 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to a report of a suspicious package delivered to a local medical facility in Palmer. Investigation revealed the package contained a flyer with religious statements and a QR code, along with three vials of suspected biohazardous material. FBI EOD technicians responded and verified the contents of the vials to be human waste and biological fluids. The package and its contents were disposed of per biohazard waste removal standards. title: AST Fatal Motor Vehicle Collision: Fairbanks [AK21106775]
summary: On 09/12/2021 at approximately 1634 hours, Alaska State Troopers Dispatch in Fairbanks received a report of a motor vehicle collision involving a motorcycle and a pickup truck at mile 94 of the Steese Highway. Investigation revealed the driver of the motorcycle was attempting to pass a pickup truck as the truck was making a turn. The driver of the motorcycle was identified as Fredrick Schenk, age 76 of Central, Alaska. After approximately an hour and a half of life saving measures by Good Samaritans on scene, Schenk succumbed to his injuries. DOT&PF from the Montana Creek station assisted with traffic control. Next of kin has been notified.

title: AST SAR/Death Investigation: Tok [AK21106567]
summary: On 9/11/21, at approximately 2119 hours, the Alaska State Troopers were notified of an SOS activation from a satellite communication device with a message stating someone had been shot and they were approximately 8.5 miles off the Taylor Highway, near Chicken, Alaska. The Alaska Air National Guard Rescue Coordination Center (AKARCC) was notified. The U.S. Army Alaska's 1st Battalion, 52nd General Support Aviation Battalion, was notified by the AKARCC and utilized a UH-60 Blackhawk to locate and transport the hunter to Ft. Wainwright. He was then transported to a Fairbanks area hospital where he was pronounced deceased. title: AST SAR: Juneau [AK21088022]
summary: On August 1, 2021 at approximately 1744 hours, the Alaska State Troopers were notified that two adult men were lost in the area of Treadwell Ditch Trail. The two men reported that they had adequate shelter, food, and water with no reported injuries, but were unable to find their way out. In consultation with Juneau Mountain Rescue, a rescue effort was planned for the morning of August 2 at first light. On August 2 at approximately 0745 hours volunteers from Juneau Mountain Rescue used a TEMSCO helicopter to search the last reported area for the two men. At approximately 0930 hours the two men were located and loaded onto the helicopter and transported back to Juneau. Neither of the two men reported injuries.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

entities.address_detail: August 1

title: AST SAR: Juneau [AK21087657]
summary: On 7/31/21 at 1756 hours, Fairbanks AST Dispatch received a report from Juneau Police Department that a 73 year-old Juneau women had not returned from a walk on Basin Road. At 2012 hours a Good Samaritan established verbal contact with the women. With the combined efforts of Alaska Seadogs, Juneau Mountain Rescue, and volunteer searchers, she was found on a hillside in thick brush next to Basin Road and was escorted out of the woods and was provided transportation to her residence. No injuries were reported and medical care was declined. Juneau Police Department was advised that she had been found. All volunteer searchers were accounted for and everyone returned from the field without further incident.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Water Rescue: Kenai [AK21008672]
summary: On July 29, 2021, at approximately 2300 hours, Alaska Wildlife Trooper Laura Reid was patrolling the North Beach during Kenai River Dipnet Fishery. Trooper Reid was making contact with dipnetters warning them of the imminent closure for the evening when she noticed a twelve-year-old child being swept down the Kenai River towards the open ocean. Trooper Reid ran into the frigid Kenai River to save the child who was barely above the water. Once Trooper Reid made it to the child, she began to float down the river as well. A good samaritan, Antoine Aridou, took notice and used his dipnet to assist Trooper Reid and the child to safety. The child was evaluated by medics and then reunited with his parents. The Alaska Wildlife Troopers would like to thank Mr. Aridou for his swift action and assist
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Aircraft Collision/ Emergency Landing: Mile 95 Parks Hwy [AK21088012]
summary: On 08/01/21 at approximately 1820 hours, AST dispatch received a call in regards to an aircraft that had to make an emergency landing on the Parks Hwy near mile 95. Upon Troopers arrival there was a single engine Seabee aircraft that was in the ditch on the northbound side of the highway. The pilot reported losing power and had to make an emergency landing. During the emergency landing the pilot struck an overhead power line. Talkeetna FD responded and secured the downed power lines. The aircraft was moved to prevent any further incidents. The FAA will conduct a full investigation.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

The Granite water truck was crucial in providing water needed for the responding fire apparatuses. title: AST Assault Investigation: Russian Mission [AK21085801]
summary: On July 28, 2021, at approximately 0315 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report that an adult male was shot in the village of Russian Mission. Troopers from Bethel and St. Mary’s attempted to respond via state aircraft in the morning, but poor weather in Russian Mission prevented their flight. Troopers were able to land in Russian Mission at approximately 1350 hours. A preliminary investigation revealed six people were returning from a boat trip at approximately 0300 hours when they came upon three males hiding in the woods waiting for them armed with long guns. A fight ensued over a shotgun, and it fired. A second shooter fired a shotgun and struck an adult male in the torso and right arm. The injured man ran and was later picked up and brought to the village clinic. The rest o
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Search and Rescue: Pioneer Ridge Trail [AK21065686]
summary: Update 6/16 1805: At approximately 1730 hours, the Alaska State Troopers were notified by a volunteer SAR member departing the search area that Fina Kiefer walked out of the woods as they were driving by on Knik River Road, about a mile from the trailhead. Kiefer has injuries and EMS is transporting her to a Mat-Su area hospital for evaluation. Update 6/16 1635: Search efforts have continued throughout the day for the missing hiker in the Pioneer Ridge Trail area with volunteer search teams from the Alaska Mountain Rescue Group, Anchorage Nordic Ski Patrol, Alaska Solstice Search Dogs, and MATSAR. Our partners from the Alaska National Guard provided aerial search assets that assisted ground searchers throughout the day in the area. No evidence of the missing hiker has been discovered. Sear
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Moose Attack: Nikiski [AK21056073]
summary: On Monday, May 24, 2021, at approximately 1841 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Soldotna were notified that 51-year-old Nikiski resident Crystal Cook had been trampled by a moose. Witnesses stated that a cow moose had been in the area for a few days without a calf, and at approximately 1000 hours on May 24, neighbors observed a newborn calf lying between Cook’s fence and an RV on the property. Neighbors stated the cow was on the other side of the fence throughout the day, observing the calf. Witnesses noted the calf called the cow in tones of distress, and Cook attempted to move toward the calf to stimulate the calf to move. At that time, the cow jumped the fence onto Cook’s property and knocked her to the ground, and began trampling her. The cow and calf left the area and headed to the
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

entities.address_detail: is fully underway throughout much of Alaska

title: AST Search and Rescue: Tuntutuliak [AK21055528]
summary: On Sunday, May 23, 2021, Alaska State Troopers in Bethel were notified by Tuntutuliak Tribal Police Officers that 22-year-old Tuntutuliak Resident David Bunyan was missing. Bunyan was last seen on Friday, May 21 delivering groceries, and failed to return to work later in the evening. Tuntutuliak Tribal Police Officers and other residents had searched the village but were not successful in locating him. Alaska State Troopers attempted to locate Bunyan using his cell phone, but efforts were not successful. As the ground search efforts continued on Monday, Bunyan was located deceased in a wooded area just outside of Tuntutuliak. No foul play is suspected. The remains were sent to the State Medical Examiner for further examination. Next of kin have been notified. *This dispatch has been update
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST MVC-F: Glacierview [AK21053395]
summary: On 5/18/21 at about 1755 hours, AST received a report of a motor vehicle collision at about mile 100 of the Glenn Highway. The Bureau of Highway Patrol responded to the scene to investigate the collision. A preliminary investigation showed that a 1998 Jeep Cherokee, operated by Derek Koso (age 39) of Eagle River was traveling southbound and crossed the centerline, and went head-on with a 2018 Jeep Compass heading northbound on the Glenn Highway. The driver, Derek Koso was transported by Lifemed to Mat-Su Regional Medical Center. The driver of the 2018 Jeep, Charis Gray (age 45) of Valdez was transported by Lifemed for her injuries. Koso died from his injuries at Mat-Su Regional. An investigation is ongoing at this time.

title: AST Sexual Abuse of a Minor: Kodiak [AK21051715]
summary: Update 5/21: On May 14, 2021, Alaska State Troopers in Kodiak were notified that 19-year-old Kodiak resident Vince Jay Barcelon Deliguin has sexually assaulted a minor multiple times over the course of a year. Kodiak Troopers initiated a thorough investigation and were able to determine that Deliguin had blackmailed the victim with the threat that he would release lewd photos of the victim if the victim did not perform sexual acts with him. When Deliguin made another attempt to sexually assault the victim, Troopers were able to arrest him at the location he arranged for the victim to meet him at. Deliguin was a volunteer volleyball team student manager at Kodiak High School, where he met the victim. Deliguin was arrested on three charges of Sexual Abuse of a Minor I, five charges of Entice
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Take Caribou Closed Season: King Cove [AK21048405]
summary: On 5/8/2021, Alaska Wildlife Troopers based In King Salmon responded to King Cove based on an anonymous tip regarding the illegal take of caribou. Investigation revealed that three caribou were taken on 5/5/2021 in GMU 09D within the Izembek NWR Wilderness Area. The season closed on 3/31/2021. Jonathan Severian (49), Graydon Severian (20), Jeremiah Wilson (23), and Howard Christiansen (75), all from King Cove were charged with taking caribou during a closed season and unlawful possession. Additionally, James Gould (73) was charged with unlawful possession. The caribou were seized and donated to charity with the firearms utilized in the offense seized as well. A summons was issued for all counts through the Anchorage Court.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

charges: taking caribou during a closed season

title: AST Bear Mauling / Search and Rescue: Glennallen [AK21053099]
summary: UPDATE: Allen Dewitt Minish, age 61 of Chitna, was surveying alone near MP 117 Richardson HWY, approximately a half mile off the roadway when he unknowingly walked up on an adult brown bear. The bear charged and attacked him causing puncture wounds and lacerations to his head. After the brief encounter, the bear left the area and there was no indication that the bear was harmed in the incident. Minish is being treated for serious injuries at an Anchorage hospital. The eluding continued to Gene Road and then back to Carver road and eventually to Frogberry Street. The driver eventually ran out of fuel and he was arrested without further incident. title: AST MVC-Fatality: Gustavus [AK21039811]
summary: On April 16, 2021, at approximately 1729 hours Alaska State Troopers in Juneau were notified of a fatal ATV accident in Gustavus. Investigation revealed that 33-year-old Juneau resident Theodore Kennedy was driving an ATV near the Gustavus Airport when he lost control and struck a concrete culvert and was ejected from the ATV. Kennedy was pronounced deceased at the scene by Gustavus EMS. Kennedy was not wearing a helmet at the time of the collision and alcohol appears to have been a factor. Next of kin have been notified and the investigation continues.

title: AST Death Investigation: Kotlik [AK21039112]
summary: On April 15, 2021, at approximately 0800 hours, Alaska State Troopers were notified that a man had been shot inside a residence in Kotlik and was deceased. Due to poor weather in the area on 4/15, Troopers from Bethel were unable to travel to Kotlik until 4/16. Based on the investigation, 49-year-old Kotlik resident Yago Pitka was inside the home of another Kotlik resident when Pitka was asked to leave; however, he refused to leave the residence. Pitka then physically attacked the resident inside his home, and a physical altercation occurred between the two individuals. The resident then produced a firearm and fired at Pitka, striking him fatally. Pitka was declared deceased at the scene. At this time, no criminal charges have been filed, and the case will be turned over to the district at
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

entities.address_detail: the investigation

title: AST Drugs: Ketchikan [AK21039224]
summary: Troopers were contacted in relation to a REDDI on North Tongass Highway at Eichner Avenue that was unable to maintain its lane while driving. Troopers located the vehicle on Eichner Avenue and contacted the driver, and sole occupant, Victor Sivertson (34 Y/O), who displayed signs of narcotics use and possession. A K9 sniff was performed on the vehicle and indicated positive to the presence of the odor of narcotics. A Search Warrant was obtained for the vehicle and served. At the conclusion of the service suspected methamphetamine and narcotics consumption evidence was seized. Sivertson was arrested on one count of Misconduct Involving a Controlled Substance in the 5th degree and remanded to Ketchikan Correctional Center.

title: AST Fatal Boating Accident: Sitka [AK21036844]
summary: Update 4/16: Alaska Wildlife Troopers have determined the cause of the fatal boating accident to be a catastrophic steering system failure that threw both occupants from the skiff into the water. One of the steering cables was found broken during an inspection after the skiff was taken out of the water. Based on a preliminary investigation by the medical examiner, Mr. Merculief’s death was caused by a combination of hypothermia and drowning. Original: On Friday, April 9, 2021, at approximately 1230 hours, Alaska State Troopers and Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Sitka were notified of two individuals being thrown from a 17’ skiff in the area of the Starrigavan Boat Launch near Lisianski Point. title: AST Search & Rescue, Agency Assist: Denali National Park [AK21034339]
summary: On 04-03-21 at approximately 1116 hours, AKRCC (Alaska Rescue Coordination Center) contacted the Alaska State Troopers to report a pending Search & Rescue situation. RCC stated 3 aircraft, a Cessna 180 and two Piper PA18, along with 5 persons were stranded on the Ruth Glacier, located at the base of Denali, inside of the Denali National Park at an altitude of approximately 4,500 feet. Further investigation revealed the group had flown to the Ruth Glacier on 04-02-21 and spent the night on the glacier after inclement weather moved in and they were unable to fly out. Due to limited survival resources, the group requested a rescue. Due to a lack of aerial resources, the National Park Service requested assistance from AST. Between AST, RCC, and the National Park Service, arrangements were made

title: AST Fail to Obtain Catcher-Processor Permit: Juneau [AK21005066]
summary: On 1/15/21, Alaska Wildlife Troopers, Juneau Post, contacted Anthony Byford, age 42 of Juneau, captain and permit-holder on the F/V Ariel, while he was conducting dockside sales of processed shrimp. title: AST DWLR: Mile 8, Kenai Spur Highway, Kenai AK [AK21025153]
location: Mile 8, Kenai Spur Highway, Kenai AK
summary: On 3-11-2021 at 0054 hours, the Soldotna AST K-9 Team observed a white 2000 Chevrolet S-10 commit a moving violation. A traffic stop was conducted, and the driver of the vehicle was identified Derrick L. Hurd (27) YOA of Kenai. An APSIN check revealed Hurd is driving on a revoked operator’s license for the original charge of DWLR. During this contact Hurd was found to be in possession of drug paraphernalia. A K-9 application was performed on Hurds vehicle. K-9 Donna indicated for the presence of the odor of controlled substances emitting from Hurds vehicle. The vehicle was impounded for investigative purposes and is currently awaiting the application of a search warrant. Hurd was transported and remanded for DWLR to Wildwood Pretrial pending O/R release.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Search & Rescue: Nancy Lake [AK21025093]
summary: On 3/10/21 at approximately 2223 hours, the Alaska State Troopers received a report for a search and rescue. Further investigation revealed, Richard Goodman II age 49, of Eagle River became stranded while snowmaching with his minor son. They were attempting to get to a state rental cabin for the weekend. Both parties were able to self-rescue and walk out. Mat Su Fire and EMS responded and were able to reach Richard and his son and provide transportion back to the main parking lot. Richard and his son were okay and opted to return in a couple of days to recover their snowmachine and camping supplies.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Ice Rescue, Iditarod Patrol: Skwentna [AK21023656]
summary: On 3-9-2021, the Alaska Wildlife Troopers Knier and Gunderson were at the Iditarod Dogsled Race checkpoint on the Skwentna River at Skwentna and observed an eight-year-old male child fall into a lead of open water and begin to be pulled by the current under the ice. The victim's 10-year-old brother was present and struggling to keep him from being pulled under the ice. Wildlife Trooper Knier and Iditarod Volunteer Doug Ramsey, of Sundance, Wyoming, sprinted to the child and pulled him from to water. The Alaska Wildlife Troopers would like to thank Mr. Ramsey for his selfless act of courage which prevented a tragic loss of life. We would also like to remind everyone who is on or near water to exercise caution and closely monitor their children. PHOTO: Trooper Knier and Mr. Ramsey standing n
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Felony Eluding/Criminal Trespass/Warrant Arrest: Trunk Road - Palmer [AK21023059]
summary: On 3/5/21, at 1358 hours, State Troopers attempted to stop a Hyundai Elantra on Trunk road for speeding. The driver, later identified as Donald Caulkins, 32 yoa of Wasilla, attemped to elude the Trooper and drove recklessly through a subdivision at high rates of speed. Caulkins eventually drove down a dead end street and into a private drive, where he fled from the vehicle on foot. After running from the Trooper a short distance, Caulkins attempted to hide inside the garage of a resident in the area. When confronted by the homeowner, Caulkins again fled and was apprehended by Troopers immediately. Investigation revealed Caulkins was on parole for numerous felonies and had multiple no-bail parole violation warrants for his arrest. Caulkins was arrested and remanded on his warrants and faces
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Burglary II, Criminal Trespass I, and Theft IV: Prince Of Wales Island [AK21022841]
incident_type: Burglary II, Criminal Trespass I, and Theft IV
summary: On 3/5/2021 at 0133 hours, Prince Of Wales AWT contacted Terry J Newkirk (54 y/o) of Washington at a un-occupied residence in Hollis. The contact showed Newkirk had burglarized the residence after a search warrant was served on the residence the previous day. He was seen exiting the residence with items that did not belong to him and he did not have permission from the person in charge of the residence to be inside it. He was arrested on one count each of Burglary II, Criminal Trespass I, and Theft IV. He was transported and remanded to the Craig Jail.

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Fairbanks [AK21017364]
summary: On February 18, 2021, at approximately 2230 hours Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks received a report that a stolen vehicle was spotted near Davis Road in Fairbanks. The vehicle had been stolen at gunpoint a few days prior by an unknown male. Troopers located the vehicle and attempted a traffic stop; the vehicle failed to yield and lead AST on a 15-mile pursuit. During the pursuit, the driver was intentionally driving head-on towards oncoming traffic and drove the wrong way on a controlled access road. After Troopers successfully utilized a PIT maneuver, the driver intentionally rammed two AST patrol vehicles, causing significant damage to both. The driver, 33-year-old Fairbanks resident Lawrence Purdue, disabled his vehicle and was taken into custody by Troopers. After receiving medical
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Akiachak [AK21017122]
summary: On February 18, 2021 at approximately 1300 hours the Akiachak Police Department notified Alaska State Troopers in Bethel about an erratic stolen white pickup truck that was enroute to Bethel. Troopers located the vehicle in Bethel and attempted a traffic stop, the vehicle failed to yield and led Troopers on a 50-mile pursuit along the ice roads between Bethel and Akiachak. Just outside of Akiachak the vehicle lost control and became stuck in a snowbank. Troopers arrested the driver, 30-year-old Akiachak resident, Charlene Peter on charges of Vehicle theft in the 1st degree, Failure to stop at the direction of a peace officer, Felony misuse of a license plates, DUI, and Reckless endangerment. Additionally, her passengers identified as 49-year-old Eddie Pasitnak of Akiachak, and 30-year-old
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Wildlife Violation Fail to Salvage: Anchorage / JBER [AK21000873]
summary: On 1-4-21, at approximately 0925 hours, Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Anchorage investigated a shot cow moose on JBER. The investigation revealed Nathaniel Theodore Goseli, age 29 of Eagle River, shot a cow moose and failed to salvage all edible meat as required for permit hunt DM421 in GMU 14C. Goseli was issued a Alaska Uniform Citation to appear in court for failure to salvage all edible meat.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST SAR: Kuskokwim River [AK21000385]
summary: On 1/2/21 at approximately 0920 hours, Troopers in Bethel were informed by the Bethel Search and Rescue that they were responding to a report of two snowmachiners that had driven into a hole in the ice on the Kuskokwim River downstream of Tuluksak. The Bethel SAR reported local residents of Tuluksak witnessed the snowmachiners drive into open water at 1935 hours on 1/1/21. On 1/4/21 two snowmachines and one body have been removed from the Kuskokwim River at the spot the two were seen driving into the hole in the ice. Searchers are continuing to search for the second individual.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Homicide: Emmonak [AK21000361]
summary: At approximately 0325 hours on January 1, 2021, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a deceased male at a residence in Emmonak. Troopers from Emmonak responded and located 39-year-old Brian Agwiak of Emmonak, deceased in his residence. Investigators from the Alaska Bureau of Investigation and Forensic Scientists from the State of Alaska Crime Lab responded to the scene to investigate. Investigation revealed that 27-year-old Rudolf Waska of Emmonak, assaulted Brian Agwiak until he was unconscious and then continued to assault him further. Agwiak later died a short time after the assault. Results from the State Medical Examiner are still pending. Waska was arrested and arraigned on charges of Murder in the 2nd Degree and Assault in the 1st Degree. Waska is being held on a $100,000 cash
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

summary: On 12/28/2020 the Alaska State Troopers were on patrol in Houston Alaska. A Trooper attempted to stop a gray sedan for driving 9 MPH over the Posted Speed in a 45 MPH zone on the Parks Highway near mile 57. The driver of the sedan failed to stop at the direction of the Trooper and the Trooper pursued the vehicle southbound. Another Trooper was able to spike the vehicle near the Gorilla Fireworks stand at mile 52 of the Parks Highway. The spike was successful, but the vehicle continued to travel southbound towards Wasilla on rims. Eventually the vehicle lost control and crashed into the ditch at mile 48.5 of the Parks Highway. sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Landslide SAR: Haines [AK20093211]
summary: Update 12/7: The Alaska State Troopers has suspended active search operations for David Simmons, age 30 of Haines and Janae Larson, age 23 of Haines due to continued rain and the likelihood of additional landslides in the area. Ground searchers deployed to Haines with the Troopers departed the area today aboard the P/V Enforcer. An Alaska State Trooper Sergeant from Juneau will remain in Haines to coordinate with the incident commander and Haines Borough Police Department on the ongoing disaster relief efforts. If new information or evidence is located Troopers will reevaluate search efforts at that time. title: AST Wanton Waste - Big Game: Soldotna - Funny River Road [AK20066345]
summary: On 9/20/2020 the Alaska Wildlife Troopers received a report of a dead bull moose in the middle of York Drive near 15 Mile Funny River Road in Soldotna. Investigation revealed that the moose had been shot twice and not salvaged. Thanks to quick reporting by nearby residents the moose was deemed salvageable and donated to a local charity. Anyone with information is requested to call the Alaska Wildlife Troopers in Soldotna at 907-262-4573 or the Alaska Wildlife Safeguard hotline at 1-800-478-3377. Callers can remain anonymous. Additionally anyone with security cameras that would have been covering the area of Funny River Road and/or York Drive on 9/20/2020 around the timeframe of 7:00 PM are encouraged to contact the Alaska Wildlife Troopers.

title: AST Death Investigation- Homicide: Minto [AK20061248]
summary: On 9/1/2020 at 1147 hours, AST received report of a death in the village of Minto. Knowland Silas, 73 of Minto, was found deceased in his residence. AST / GIU responded to Minto to conduct the investigation. The cause of death was not obvious and Silas’ body was transported to the State Medical Examiner’s Office in Anchorage. Following the autopsy, Silas’ death was classified as a homicide. On 9/10/2020, AST responded to Minto to conduct follow-up investigation and after consulting with the Fairbanks DAO, Zachary Charlie, 27 of Minto, was arrested and remanded for Criminally Negligent Homicide.

title: AST Human Remains Found: Aniak [AK20063071]
summary: On 9/6/2020 at approximately 1435 hours, Aniak AST Troopers received a call from a Knik Construction Employee reporting that during excavations for the new electric lines at the Aniak Airport, what appeared to be a human jaw bone was unearthed. Aniak AST responded to the location and confirmed the remains were in fact a human. Upon further investigation, more bones were located. The state medical examiner’s office was contacted about the incident. At this time, it is believed to be possibly an old burial site. The remains were collected, photographs taken and the remains are being sent to the SME in Anchorage for examination. Investigation is ongoing.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

The incident occurred sometime between yesterday evening and this morning when the store was closed. The estimated damage to the building is approximately $1000.00. Investigation is continuing. Anyone with information should contact the Alaska State Troopers.

title: AST Taking deer closed season: Prince of Wales Island [AK20028661]
summary: On 4/27/20, Alaska Wildlife Troopers on Prince of Wales Island were notified of several deer that had been taken illegally during the closed season. AWT responded to the area and located four gut-piles, each with an unborn fawn, as well as, the heads and lower legs of four adult female deer. The deer appeared to have been recently killed (within the past 12 hours). The deer had likely been shot elsewhere and then transported to the remote log landing on Saltery Point near Hydaburg, where they were gutted and processed. Several items of evidence were located at the scene, and the investigation is ongoing. Anyone with information about this incident is encouraged to contact the Alaska Wildlife Troopers-Prince of Wales post at 907-826-2291.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive

title: AST Snowmachine/Musher collision: Galena [AK20015147]
summary: Update: The driver of snowmachine contacted AST and is cooperating with the ongoing investigation. Original Text: On 3/2/2020, Alaska State Troopers in Galena received a report that a musher who was participating in the Serum Run was struck the night prior, at approximately 2030 hours, by a snowmachiner about five miles outside of Galena. Investigation revealed that Kirsten Bey, 64 of Nome, was mushing to Galena from Ruby when she was struck by an unknown snowmachiner. Another participant, riding snowmachine, came upon the scene shortly after and helped transport Bey to Galena. The snowmachiner that hit the musher remained on scene until Bey was transported away. Bey was medivaced from Galena to the Fairbanks Medical Hospital with significant injuries. The incident is under investigation.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Historical Archive
